questions.text: Was the Quran revealed to cause the Prophet (SAW) distress?
questions.answer: No, Verse 2 says: 'We have not sent down to you the Quran that you be distressed'.

questions.text: Where did Musa (AS) first perceive the fire and hear the voice of Allah?
questions.answer: In the direction of Mount Tur (the valley of Tuwa).

questions.text: What was Musa (AS) commanded to do when he approached the burning bush?
questions.answer: To remove his sandals, for he was in the sacred valley of Tuwa.

questions.text: What were the two miracles given to Musa (AS) at the start of his mission?
questions.answer: His staff turning into a serpent and his hand shining white without disease.

questions.text: What was the specific prayer Musa (AS) made to Allah before facing Pharaoh?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, expand for me my breast, ease for me my task, and untie the knot from my tongue...'

questions.text: Why did Musa (AS) ask for his brother Harun to accompany him?
questions.answer: To be his helper and to share in his task, because Harun was more eloquent in speech.

questions.text: How did Allah protect Musa (AS) as an infant when he was placed in the chest in the river?
questions.answer: Allah cast love upon him from Himself and ensured he was raised under His eye in Pharaoh's own palace.

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's reaction to the signs shown by Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: He accused him of being a magician and challenged him to a public duel with his own magicians.

questions.text: What happened to the magicians when they saw Musa's staff swallow their illusions?
questions.answer: They fell down in prostration, declaring their belief in the Lord of Harun and Musa.

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's threat to the magicians after they believed?
questions.answer: To cut off their hands and feet on opposite sides and crucify them on the trunks of palm trees.

questions.text: What miracle occurred when the Children of Israel reached the sea with Pharaoh in pursuit?
questions.answer: Allah commanded Musa to strike the sea, creating a dry path for them to cross.

questions.text: Who was Samiri and what did he do in Musa's absence?
questions.answer: He was a man who misled the Children of Israel into worshipping a golden calf made from their jewelry.

questions.text: How did Harun (AS) react to the people worshipping the calf?
questions.answer: He tried to stop them, reminding them that Allah is Ar-Rahman, but they refused to listen until Musa returned.

questions.text: What did Musa (AS) do with the golden calf when he returned?
questions.answer: He burned it and scattered its dust into the sea.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who turn away from the Quran as mentioned in Verse 124?
questions.answer: They will have a 'depressed life' (Ma'ishatan Dhanka) and be raised blind on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What will happen to the mountains on the Day of Judgment according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah will blast them into dust, leaving the earth a level plain with no curves or hills.

questions.text: How is the story of Adam and Iblis summarized in the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Iblis refused to prostrate; Adam was warned about him but forgot the command and ate from the tree, leading to their descent to earth.

questions.text: What is the instruction to the Prophet (SAW) regarding the timing of prayer (Verse 130)?
questions.answer: To exalt Allah before the rising of the sun and before its setting, and during the hours of the night and at the ends of the day.

questions.text: Is the Prophet (SAW) commanded to force his family to pray?
questions.answer: He is commanded to enjoin prayer on his family and be steadfast in it himself.

questions.text: What is the 'clear proof' mentioned in Verse 133 that the disbelievers demanded?
questions.answer: The previous scriptures (like the Torah and Gospel) which contain the same core message as the Quran.
